title: Docker Registry Mirror Health
dataSources: `sourcetype=harbor:nginx:access`, `sourcetype=artifactory:request`, `sourcetype=nexus:access` for registry HTTP access logs; `sourcetype=harbor:job_service` for Harbor garbage-collection and maintenance events; `sourcetype=linux:df` for mirror-side filesystem fill ratio from Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux; `index=oti_registry`; join-wrapped `inputlookup` on `lookups/registry_mirror_inventory.csv` supplying mirror_hostname, mirror_type, upstream_registry, expected_cache_hit_pct, and capacity_threshold_pct.
app: Splunk Add-on for Nginx or Harbor-specific ingress forwarding for harbor:nginx:access; Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (Splunkbase 833) for linux:df scripted inputs; JFrog Splunk App or HTTP Event Collector patterns for artifactory:request; Sonatype Nexus Splunk integration or monitored request logs for nexus:access; optional Splunk Add-on for Amazon Web Services if CloudWatch–backed ECR cache telemetry complements on-host logs.
spl:
`comment("UC-3.1.20 Docker Registry Mirror Health. Tunables: index=oti_registry; join registry_mirror_inventory.csv; five_xx_critical_pct=5; p99_high_ms=5000; cache_hit_med_pct=80; cache_hit_low_pct=60; disk_crit_pct=90; earliest=-1h@h latest=@h.")`
| search index=oti_registry (sourcetype="harbor:nginx:access" OR sourcetype="artifactory:request" OR sourcetype="nexus:access" OR sourcetype="harbor:job_service" OR sourcetype="linux:df") earliest=-1h@h latest=@h
| eval mirror_hostname=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(mirror_host, mirrorHost, dest, host, http_host, server_name, registry_node, fqdn, ""))))
| eval arm=case(sourcetype="harbor:job_service","gc", sourcetype="linux:df","df", true(),"http")
| eval status=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(status, http_status, sc_status, response_code, responseStatus, "")), 10)
| eval rt_num=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(request_time, request_time_sec, request_duration, time_taken, upstream_response_time, "")), 10)
| eval response_time_ms=if(isnotnull(rt_num) AND rt_num<=500, round(rt_num*1000, 2), coalesce(rt_num, tonumber(tostring(coalesce(response_time_ms, duration_ms, total_service_time, "")), 10)))
| eval is_5xx=if(arm="http" AND isnotnull(status) AND status>=500 AND status<600, 1, 0)
| eval lr=lower(_raw)
| eval cache_hit=if(arm="http" AND match(lr, "(?i)cache[_ -]hit|x-cache:\s*hit|\bh\b\s*:\s*hit|edge[_ -]hit|\bhit\b"), 1, 0)
| eval cache_miss=if(arm="http" AND match(lr, "(?i)cache[_ -]miss|x-cache:\s*miss|upstream|revalidate|tcp[_ -]miss|parent|\bmiss\b"), 1, 0)
| eval gc_evt=if(arm="gc" AND match(lr, "(?i)garbage[_ ]collection|\bgc\b|delete[_ ]untagged|sweep[_ ]blob|online[_ ]gc"), 1, 0)
| eval disk_fill_pct=if(arm="df", tonumber(replace(tostring(coalesce(PercentUsedSpace, pctUsed, UsePercent, use_pcent, PCT_USED, "")), "[^0-9.]", ""), 10), null())
| where len(mirror_hostname)>2
| streamstats window=240 current=t global=f sum(eval(if(arm="http", cache_miss, 0))) AS cmiss sum(eval(if(arm="http", cache_hit, 0))) AS chit BY mirror_hostname
| eval running_miss_ratio=if((chit+cmiss)>0, round(100.0*cmiss/(chit+cmiss), 4), null())
| streamstats window=3 current=t global=f delta(running_miss_ratio) AS cache_miss_rate_delta BY mirror_hostname
| bin _time span=60m aligntime=@h
| stats
    count(eval(if(arm="http", 1, null()))) AS http_req
    sum(eval(if(arm="http", is_5xx, 0))) AS http_5xx
    perc99(eval(if(arm="http", response_time_ms, null()))) AS p99_response_time_ms
    sum(eval(if(arm="http", cache_hit, 0))) AS cache_hits
    sum(eval(if(arm="http", cache_miss, 0))) AS cache_misses
    sum(eval(if(arm="gc", gc_evt, 0))) AS gc_event_count
    max(eval(if(arm="df", disk_fill_pct, null()))) AS disk_fill_pct
    max(abs(cache_miss_rate_delta)) AS miss_delta_peak
  BY mirror_hostname
| eval http_5xx_rate_pct=if(http_req>0, round(100.0*http_5xx/http_req, 3), 0)
| eval denom=cache_hits+cache_misses
| eval cache_hit_pct=if(denom>0, round(100.0*cache_hits/denom, 2), null())
| fillnull value=0 gc_event_count
| fillnull value=0 http_5xx_rate_pct
| fillnull value=0 disk_fill_pct
| join type=left max=0 mirror_hostname
    [| inputlookup registry_mirror_inventory.csv
     | eval mirror_hostname=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(mirror_hostname, mirror_host, hostname, ""))))
     | eval mirror_type=toString(coalesce(mirror_type, mirrorType, vendor_class, product_family, ""))
     | eval upstream_registry=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(upstream_registry, upstream, remote_registry, ""))))
     | eval expected_cache_hit_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(expected_cache_hit_pct, expected_hit_pct, golden_hit_pct, baseline_hit_pct, "")), 10)
     | eval capacity_threshold_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(capacity_threshold_pct, disk_warn_threshold_pct, fs_warn_pct, "")), 10)
     | fields mirror_hostname mirror_type upstream_registry expected_cache_hit_pct capacity_threshold_pct ]
| eval expected_cache_hit_from_baseline=coalesce(expected_cache_hit_pct, 85.0)
| eval capacity_threshold_pct=coalesce(capacity_threshold_pct, 80.0)
| eventstats avg(p99_response_time_ms) AS fleet_avg_p99_for_type BY mirror_type
| eval severity=case(
    http_5xx_rate_pct>5.0, "critical_mirror_5xx_rate_above_5pct",
    disk_fill_pct>90.0, "critical_mirror_disk_above_90pct",
    p99_response_time_ms>5000.0, "high_mirror_p99_above_5s",
    cache_hit_pct<60.0, "high_cache_hit_below_60pct",
    cache_hit_pct<80.0, "medium_cache_hit_below_80pct",
    isnull(mirror_type) OR len(trim(mirror_type))==0 OR isnull(upstream_registry) OR len(trim(upstream_registry))==0, "low_mirror_inventory_drift",
    true(), null())
| where isnotnull(severity)
| eval recommended_response=case(
    severity="critical_mirror_5xx_rate_above_5pct", "Shift clients to alternate mirror VIP; snapshot ingress error_log; verify registry-backend pod readiness; inspect WAF or API gateway 5xx mapping; open joint bridge with network and identity teams.",
    severity="critical_mirror_disk_above_90pct", "Freeze GC experiments; add blob-storage volumes; verify soft-quota and tag-retention schedules; drain heavy CI promotion traffic; plan emergency layer eviction with vendor guidance.",
    severity="high_mirror_p99_above_5s", "Trace manifest versus blob latency; check storage fabric queue-depth; validate anti-virus scanning hooks; compare p99 to sibling mirrors sharing upstream_registry.",
    severity="high_cache_hit_below_60pct", "Expect upstream token storms—pre-seed golden digests; raise remote-repo concurrency; inspect rate-limit telemetry on docker.io; shift CI to authenticated pull identities.",
    severity="medium_cache_hit_below_80pct", "Refresh edge cache rules; review Harbor proxy or Artifactory remote metadata TTL; schedule off-hours base-image rebuild coordination.",
    severity="low_mirror_inventory_drift", "Regenerate registry_mirror_inventory.csv; reconcile mirror_hostname with Splunk host naming; document temporary lab mirrors separately.",
    true(), "Compare harbor:nginx:access, harbor:job_service, and linux:df ingestion gaps before closure.")
| table mirror_hostname mirror_type upstream_registry http_5xx_rate_pct p99_response_time_ms cache_hit_pct disk_fill_pct gc_event_count severity recommended_response expected_cache_hit_from_baseline fleet_avg_p99_for_type
description: Unique monitoring axis: Fortune-scale Docker estates that point two hundred or more hosts at internal registry mirrors depend on mirror-tier health more than on any single worker daemon. When Harbor proxy cache, Artifactory remote repository, Nexus pull-through, or ECR cache endpoints degrade, manifests and blobs still answer HTTP but tail latency spreads randomly across the fleet because every dockerd competes for the same weakened cache path. This use case fuses three telemetry planes from the registry server perspective: normalized HTTP access logs with OCI path awareness so 404 manifest churn, 206 blob resumes, and 5xx upstream faults classify correctly; cache HIT versus MISS semantics so operators see when pull-through stops insulating docker.io from burst traffic; and GC plus linux:df signals so disk pressure and long delete phases explain blocking behavior before client-side pull logs do. UC-3.1.26 remains the north-south dockerd journal and metrics story for client pull failures, auth, DNS, and Hub rate-limit headers. UC-3.1.14 stays focused on Docker overlay east-west control-plane issues. Neither replaces mirror-side nginx, job service, or blob-store capacity telemetry joined to registry_mirror_inventory.csv.
value: Quantified outcomes begin with avoided Docker Hub anonymous rate-limit storms: when cache_hit_pct falls below eighty percent, Splunk shows the mirror that stopped absorbing upstream fetches, giving FinOps evidence to fund more cache disk or authenticated Hub entitlements before CI brownouts. Deployment pipeline latency drops when p99 mirror response times are tracked alongside 5xx ratios because platform teams can fail over VIPs or burst read replicas while application teams still think pulls are random. SOC 2 CC9.2 vendor and dependency management reviews gain timestamped exports proving you monitor internal registry mirrors, not only SaaS endpoints. Regulatory narratives improve when you can demonstrate quarterly stability of mirror HTTP classes, GC cadence, and filesystem headroom for the same artifact path your SDLC relies on. Customer trust increases because executives see recommended_response text that names mirror failover, cache warming, and storage expansion instead of generic restart guidance that repeats failing pulls.
implementation: Ingest harbor:nginx:access, artifactory:request, nexus:access, harbor:job_service, and linux:df into index=oti_registry; publish lookups/registry_mirror_inventory.csv; schedule container_uc_3_1_20_registry_mirror_health every five minutes; route critical severities to registry SRE and archive weekly evidence CSV snapshots with lookup version hashes.
visualization: Mirror p99 heatmap faceted by mirror_type, cache_hit_pct time series with fleet and baseline ribbons, top ten mirrors by http_5xx_rate_pct bar chart, and disk_fill_pct trend panels split by upstream_registry.
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=true count FROM datamodel=Web WHERE nodename=Web earliest=-1h@h latest=@h BY Web.url Web.status span=5m
| rename Web.url AS uri Web.status AS status
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Splunk CIM Web and Performance summaries on normalized registry access feeds when you map uri, status, and response_time into the Web data model; retain thirty-day summaries for quarterly vendor reviews.

references.title: Docker Docs — Docker Hub download rate limit
Shopify Engineering — Docker at Shopify (registry, nginx caching, deploy herds)
Splunk Lantern — Getting Docker log data into Splunk Cloud Platform with OpenTelemetry
AWS Documentation — Using pull through cache repositories in Amazon ECR

hardware: Targets x86_64 and ARM64 Linux registry nodes running Harbor on Kubernetes or VMs, JFrog Artifactory cluster members, Sonatype Nexus Repository JVM hosts, and AWS ECR pull-through cache infrastructure in front of S3-backed storage. Typical hardware includes NVMe-backed blob volumes, ten-gigabit front-end NICs, and dual-socket servers sized for concurrent layer extraction during promotion waves. Air-gapped estates mirror the same logging expectations onto ruggedized forwarders with offline lookup refresh processes.
exclusions: Excludes dev and test mirrors that intentionally disable cache for vulnerability reproduction, mirrors inside approved maintenance windows where GC pauses are expected, isolated air-gapped registry replicas with no upstream docker.io path, single-tenant Harbor projects without pull-through semantics where cache_hit_pct is undefined, and client-only diagnostics already covered by UC-3.1.26.
knownFalsePositives: A fresh golden base rebuild that invalidates manifests can depress cache_hit_pct for one hour without indicating broken mirrors; annotate change records and compare against expected_cache_hit_from_baseline. Scheduled Harbor or Nexus garbage collection produces intentional latency spikes and temporary 503 responses; exclude documented maintenance windows or raise thresholds during those slices. Rolling upgrades of registry pods legitimately generate short 5xx bursts while health checks flap; correlate with deployment pipelines before paging. CI runners that always pull unique digests for inner-loop builds create chronic MISS traffic that looks like cache failure but reflects workflow design, not mirror health. OCI artifacts such as Helm charts or cosign signatures may bypass traditional layer-cache accounting, skewing cache_hit_pct until you add artifact-type filters. Regional failover that shifts pull traffic to a secondary datacenter can elevate p99 while caches warm; treat as expected if geography tags in the lookup explain the shift. When mirrors correctly proxy Docker Hub rate limits, clients may see HTTP 429 with structured bodies; classify those separately from 5xx upstream failures so identity and quota teams own the response instead of storage teams. Artifactory smart remote repositories sometimes log upstream timeouts as 404 or 409 depending on version; tune rex extractions so status classes remain trustworthy.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the regional warehouses that keep extra copies of popular books so nearby libraries can lend them quickly. If a warehouse runs out of shelf space or its sorting machine jams, wait times jump all over the city even when each local library looks healthy from the street.
evidence: Evidence bundles combine OCI Distribution Specification excerpts, Docker Hub rate-limit documentation, Harbor and AWS ECR pull-through cache guides, Shopify engineering registry-and-proxy architecture narratives, Splunk Lantern Docker OpenTelemetry ingestion guidance, and scheduled exports of container_uc_3_1_20_registry_mirror_health with registry_mirror_inventory.csv version identifiers for auditors.
detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control with registry site reliability engineers and the artifact-management team that operates Harbor, JFrog Artifactory, Sonatype Nexus, and Amazon ECR pull-through cache endpoints. Unique monitoring axis: mirror-tier infrastructure health observed from the registry server side—HTTP semantics on OCI Distribution paths, cache effectiveness of pull-through and proxy repositories, garbage-collection pressure, and filesystem headroom for blob stores—rather than dockerd client pull errors (UC-3.1.26) or overlay control-plane packet loss (UC-3.1.14). You need Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud, a dedicated index such as oti_registry, and RBAC roles that let mirror operators read production registry indexes without exposing customer image names to every developer workspace.

Publish lookups/registry_mirror_inventory.csv with mirror_hostname keyed the same way Universal Forwarders populate host or dest, plus mirror_type, upstream_registry, expected_cache_hit_pct, and capacity_threshold_pct. Refresh the lookup weekly from your configuration-management database so drift detection stays honest. Splunk Add-on for Nginx (or vendor-specific Harbor ingress packaging) should land harbor:nginx:access with status, timing, and URI fields. JFrog and Nexus access logs map to artifactory:request and nexus:access through their Splunk connectors or scripted file tails. Harbor job service lines that mention garbage collection belong in harbor:job_service. Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux scripted inputs or df monitoring should emit linux:df rows from every mirror node that stores layers, not only the management VM.

Capacity planning: registry HTTP logs are high volume during release days; retain at least fourteen days hot for percentile math and keep a longer frozen archive if auditors ask for quarter-over-quarter mirror stability. Coordinate with security so TLS termination logs do not duplicate the same request at edge and origin without a dedup key. Document which mirrors are pull-through caches versus authoritative registries because cache_hit_pct math applies only where upstream fetches are expected to be a minority of requests.

Licensing: heavy use of perc99 and streamstats across wide time windows belongs on a scheduled search or report acceleration, not interactive-only dashboards, to protect search concurrency.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

On Harbor front nodes, enable nginx access logging with upstream_response_time or request_time compatible fields and ensure Splunk reads the file with sourcetype harbor:nginx:access. Normalize server_name or host to mirror_hostname in props.conf when the default host field is the forwarder instead of the registry VIP. For Artifactory, route request logs through the official Splunk integration or a monitored path under $JFROG_HOME with sourcetype artifactory:request; verify response codes for /v2/* manifest and blob paths appear. For Nexus Repository 3, enable HTTP request logging to disk and ingest with sourcetype nexus:access, preserving duration and status columns.

Ship harbor:job_service from Harbor core or job pods with the same mirror_hostname tagging used for nginx so GC bursts correlate with disk metrics. On each Linux mirror host, enable the Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux df scripted input targeting the filesystem that backs blob storage, emitting PercentUsedSpace or equivalent into sourcetype linux:df. Map mount points in a small lookup if one VM serves both PostgreSQL and blob disks so you alert on the data volume, not the root file system.

Field extraction guardrails: OCI clients mix GET /v2/<name>/manifests/<reference> and GET /v2/<name>/blobs/<digest>; keep uri in access logs so investigators can split manifest storms from blob re-downloads. If your edge strips cache headers, add a custom header at the mirror that records HIT or MISS so cache_hit_pct is computable; without that signal the search still runs but cache_hit_pct becomes unreliable and you should mark the mirror as not_applicable in the inventory lookup until headers exist.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as container_uc_3_1_20_registry_mirror_health with a five-minute or fifteen-minute schedule and earliest=-1h@h latest=@h for operational alerting, widening to twenty-four hours for weekly executive roll-ups. Throttle duplicate pages per mirror_hostname for thirty minutes unless severity escalates from medium to critical. Route critical_mirror tiers to the registry bridge chat; route medium_cache_hit_below_80pct to artifact governance for tuning reviews.

Understanding this SPL without repeating a mechanical pipeline essay: the comment macro lists every index, sourcetype, lookup, and threshold so on-call engineers retune without opening this document. The opening search unifies five sourcetype families so one correlation addresses HTTP behavior, GC churn, and disk fill on the same mirror_hostname key. coalesce on mirror_host, mirrorHost, and dest absorbs naming differences between nginx, vendor logs, and df inputs.

streamstats maintains cumulative cache_miss and cache_hit counters per mirror_hostname over the window, then derives running_miss_ratio and a short delta to surface sudden cache-miss storms that often precede Hub rate-limit exhaustion. The hourly bin and stats stage collapses noise while preserving perc99 of response_time_ms for tail latency that docker clients feel as ImagePull stalls even when mean latency looks fine.

The join wraps inputlookup registry_mirror_inventory.csv rather than a bare lookup command so governance reviewers see the same pattern as other gold UCs and so you can extend the subsearch with eval logic for staging mirrors. eventstats avg(p99_response_time_ms) BY mirror_type adds fleet_avg_p99_for_type so a single slow mirror stands out against its technology class. severity encodes the exact tier strings required for SOAR playbooks, and recommended_response gives pre-written runbook language tied to each tier.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-3.1.20 Docker Registry Mirror Health. Tunables: index=oti_registry; join registry_mirror_inventory.csv; five_xx_critical_pct=5; p99_high_ms=5000; cache_hit_med_pct=80; cache_hit_low_pct=60; disk_crit_pct=90; earliest=-1h@h latest=@h.")`
| search index=oti_registry (sourcetype="harbor:nginx:access" OR sourcetype="artifactory:request" OR sourcetype="nexus:access" OR sourcetype="harbor:job_service" OR sourcetype="linux:df") earliest=-1h@h latest=@h
| eval mirror_hostname=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(mirror_host, mirrorHost, dest, host, http_host, server_name, registry_node, fqdn, ""))))
| eval arm=case(sourcetype="harbor:job_service","gc", sourcetype="linux:df","df", true(),"http")
| eval status=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(status, http_status, sc_status, response_code, responseStatus, "")), 10)
| eval rt_num=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(request_time, request_time_sec, request_duration, time_taken, upstream_response_time, "")), 10)
| eval response_time_ms=if(isnotnull(rt_num) AND rt_num<=500, round(rt_num*1000, 2), coalesce(rt_num, tonumber(tostring(coalesce(response_time_ms, duration_ms, total_service_time, "")), 10)))
| eval is_5xx=if(arm="http" AND isnotnull(status) AND status>=500 AND status<600, 1, 0)
| eval lr=lower(_raw)
| eval cache_hit=if(arm="http" AND match(lr, "(?i)cache[_ -]hit|x-cache:\s*hit|\bh\b\s*:\s*hit|edge[_ -]hit|\bhit\b"), 1, 0)
| eval cache_miss=if(arm="http" AND match(lr, "(?i)cache[_ -]miss|x-cache:\s*miss|upstream|revalidate|tcp[_ -]miss|parent|\bmiss\b"), 1, 0)
| eval gc_evt=if(arm="gc" AND match(lr, "(?i)garbage[_ ]collection|\bgc\b|delete[_ ]untagged|sweep[_ ]blob|online[_ ]gc"), 1, 0)
| eval disk_fill_pct=if(arm="df", tonumber(replace(tostring(coalesce(PercentUsedSpace, pctUsed, UsePercent, use_pcent, PCT_USED, "")), "[^0-9.]", ""), 10), null())
| where len(mirror_hostname)>2
| streamstats window=240 current=t global=f sum(eval(if(arm="http", cache_miss, 0))) AS cmiss sum(eval(if(arm="http", cache_hit, 0))) AS chit BY mirror_hostname
| eval running_miss_ratio=if((chit+cmiss)>0, round(100.0*cmiss/(chit+cmiss), 4), null())
| streamstats window=3 current=t global=f delta(running_miss_ratio) AS cache_miss_rate_delta BY mirror_hostname
| bin _time span=60m aligntime=@h
| stats
    count(eval(if(arm="http", 1, null()))) AS http_req
    sum(eval(if(arm="http", is_5xx, 0))) AS http_5xx
    perc99(eval(if(arm="http", response_time_ms, null()))) AS p99_response_time_ms
    sum(eval(if(arm="http", cache_hit, 0))) AS cache_hits
    sum(eval(if(arm="http", cache_miss, 0))) AS cache_misses
    sum(eval(if(arm="gc", gc_evt, 0))) AS gc_event_count
    max(eval(if(arm="df", disk_fill_pct, null()))) AS disk_fill_pct
    max(abs(cache_miss_rate_delta)) AS miss_delta_peak
  BY mirror_hostname
| eval http_5xx_rate_pct=if(http_req>0, round(100.0*http_5xx/http_req, 3), 0)
| eval denom=cache_hits+cache_misses
| eval cache_hit_pct=if(denom>0, round(100.0*cache_hits/denom, 2), null())
| fillnull value=0 gc_event_count
| fillnull value=0 http_5xx_rate_pct
| fillnull value=0 disk_fill_pct
| join type=left max=0 mirror_hostname
    [| inputlookup registry_mirror_inventory.csv
     | eval mirror_hostname=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(mirror_hostname, mirror_host, hostname, ""))))
     | eval mirror_type=toString(coalesce(mirror_type, mirrorType, vendor_class, product_family, ""))
     | eval upstream_registry=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(upstream_registry, upstream, remote_registry, ""))))
     | eval expected_cache_hit_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(expected_cache_hit_pct, expected_hit_pct, golden_hit_pct, baseline_hit_pct, "")), 10)
     | eval capacity_threshold_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(capacity_threshold_pct, disk_warn_threshold_pct, fs_warn_pct, "")), 10)
     | fields mirror_hostname mirror_type upstream_registry expected_cache_hit_pct capacity_threshold_pct ]
| eval expected_cache_hit_from_baseline=coalesce(expected_cache_hit_pct, 85.0)
| eval capacity_threshold_pct=coalesce(capacity_threshold_pct, 80.0)
| eventstats avg(p99_response_time_ms) AS fleet_avg_p99_for_type BY mirror_type
| eval severity=case(
    http_5xx_rate_pct>5.0, "critical_mirror_5xx_rate_above_5pct",
    disk_fill_pct>90.0, "critical_mirror_disk_above_90pct",
    p99_response_time_ms>5000.0, "high_mirror_p99_above_5s",
    cache_hit_pct<60.0, "high_cache_hit_below_60pct",
    cache_hit_pct<80.0, "medium_cache_hit_below_80pct",
    isnull(mirror_type) OR len(trim(mirror_type))==0 OR isnull(upstream_registry) OR len(trim(upstream_registry))==0, "low_mirror_inventory_drift",
    true(), null())
| where isnotnull(severity)
| eval recommended_response=case(
    severity="critical_mirror_5xx_rate_above_5pct", "Shift clients to alternate mirror VIP; snapshot ingress error_log; verify registry-backend pod readiness; inspect WAF or API gateway 5xx mapping; open joint bridge with network and identity teams.",
    severity="critical_mirror_disk_above_90pct", "Freeze GC experiments; add blob-storage volumes; verify soft-quota and tag-retention schedules; drain heavy CI promotion traffic; plan emergency layer eviction with vendor guidance.",
    severity="high_mirror_p99_above_5s", "Trace manifest versus blob latency; check storage fabric queue-depth; validate anti-virus scanning hooks; compare p99 to sibling mirrors sharing upstream_registry.",
    severity="high_cache_hit_below_60pct", "Expect upstream token storms—pre-seed golden digests; raise remote-repo concurrency; inspect rate-limit telemetry on docker.io; shift CI to authenticated pull identities.",
    severity="medium_cache_hit_below_80pct", "Refresh edge cache rules; review Harbor proxy or Artifactory remote metadata TTL; schedule off-hours base-image rebuild coordination.",
    severity="low_mirror_inventory_drift", "Regenerate registry_mirror_inventory.csv; reconcile mirror_hostname with Splunk host naming; document temporary lab mirrors separately.",
    true(), "Compare harbor:nginx:access, harbor:job_service, and linux:df ingestion gaps before closure.")
| table mirror_hostname mirror_type upstream_registry http_5xx_rate_pct p99_response_time_ms cache_hit_pct disk_fill_pct gc_event_count severity recommended_response expected_cache_hit_from_baseline fleet_avg_p99_for_type
```

Mirror operators should treat harbor:nginx:access as the authoritative user-perceived latency plane because that is where TLS termination, gzip, and chunked transfer interact with client-visible stalls. When Harbor sits behind an external load balancer, either move logging to the balancer with identical field names or duplicate the VIP hostname into mirror_hostname using transforms so the search does not fragment one logical mirror into two keys. Artifactory exposes request logs with slightly different column orders between OSS and Pro; normalize in props.conf so status always maps to a numeric field before this SPL executes. Nexus access logs may omit milliseconds; if so, derive response_time_ms from duration fields expressed in microseconds by dividing when values exceed ten thousand.

For Amazon ECR pull-through cache, some teams only have CloudWatch ApplicationELB access logs rather than on-instance nginx. In that pattern, clone this SPL with a parallel sourcetype arm that maps elb_status_code to status and target_processing_time to response_time_ms, and keep mirror_hostname as the cache repository endpoint DNS name. The inventory lookup should still record upstream_registry as docker.io or mcr.microsoft.com so fleet_avg_p99_for_type comparisons stay meaningful across technology classes.

The streamstats window of two hundred forty events is a starting point for dense mirrors; quiet mirrors may never fill the window, which is acceptable because running_miss_ratio still updates whenever requests arrive. If you observe false spikes after forwarder cold starts, add a workflow action that clears cumulative state by tagging host restart events from linux:audit or syslog and resetting with a subsearch that masks the first five minutes post-boot.

eventstats avg(p99_response_time_ms) BY mirror_type intentionally averages per-mirror p99 values rather than recomputing a global percentile across raw events, because the latter would be computationally expensive in this correlation and because executives want to know whether a mirror is worse than its peers of the same vendor class. If you prefer median instead of mean for robustness, replace avg with median in one edit.

The severity ladder prioritizes customer-visible outage semantics: widespread 5xx means fail over now, disk above ninety percent risks immediate read-only transitions, tail latency above five seconds aligns with docker pull timeouts in many estates, cache_hit below sixty percent predicts imminent Hub throttling, and cache_hit below eighty percent is the early warning band for platform engineering. low_mirror_inventory_drift exists so CMDB negligence does not silently orphan telemetry; fix the CSV instead of muting the alert.

Dashboard drilldowns should link mirror_hostname to your runbook that lists kubectl contexts, Artifactory service IDs, or AWS account numbers so on-call does not grep spreadsheets during incidents. Maintain a secondary panel that plots miss_delta_peak so operations can see the derivative of cache miss pressure even when absolute cache_hit_pct still looks acceptable.

Training scenarios should include a table-top where dockerd clients show no errors yet harbor:nginx:access p99 climbs because disk latency increased; this use case is how you win that argument without waiting for UC-3.1.26 client errors to appear. Pair quarterly reviews with finance by translating minutes of additional pull latency into pipeline SLA breaches using your internal DORA metrics.

Governance: when mirror_hostname keys change during cloud migrations, version the registry_mirror_inventory.csv in git and reference commit hashes in evidence exports. When legal asks whether PII exists in registry logs, scrub user-agent strings or disable them at source per counsel guidance while retaining timing and status.

Performance tuning: if stats BY mirror_hostname after bin spans more than ten thousand mirrors, pre-aggregate into a summary index with collect or a scheduled transform. If join to inventory explodes because CSV contains historical retired hosts, filter retired=false in the subsearch.

Reliability: mirror-tier incidents often coincide with certificate rotations; keep ssl_termination renewal dates in the lookup as optional columns even though this SPL does not yet consume them, so future versions can join without another migration.

Closing differentiation checklist: confirm you are not duplicating UC-3.1.26 docker:journald pull failures, UC-3.1.14 overlay UDP issues, UC-3.1.8 broad dockerd errors without registry classifiers, or UC-3.1.11 daemon ulimit starvation that is local to each worker.

Step 4 — Validate

Positive path A: replay a captured harbor:nginx:access slice where status 502 spikes above five percent of rows for one mirror_hostname; confirm severity critical_mirror_5xx_rate_above_5pct and non-zero http_5xx_rate_pct.

Positive path B: inject synthetic linux:df rows with PercentUsedSpace ninety-two on a lab mirror; confirm critical_mirror_disk_above_90pct even when http_req is zero so disk-only failure modes still page.

Positive path C: manufacture artifactory:request lines with MISS headers dominating HIT for an hour; confirm medium_cache_hit_below_80pct or high_cache_hit_below_60pct depending on ratio, and verify expected_cache_hit_from_baseline comes from the lookup coalesce default of eighty-five when the CSV omits the column.

Negative path D: healthy mirror with cache_hit_pct eighty-eight and p99 under five hundred milliseconds should produce null severity and be filtered out by where isnotnull(severity); if you need a health dashboard, clone the search without the final where and without the severity case.

Performance: run Job Inspector on the first full day of production traffic; if join fan-in exceeds budget, materialize an hourly summary index with outputlookup and point this search at the summary.

Role check: readers without index oti_registry must see zero rows.

Step 5 — Operationalize and troubleshoot

Case 1 — cache_hit_pct always null: access logs lack HIT or MISS markers; add edge headers or switch to vendor metrics export for hit ratio until HTTP logs carry the signal.

Case 2 — streamstats delta explodes on forwarder restarts: bound window or add filldown after host reboot events so cumulative counters reset predictably.

Case 3 — p99_response_time_ms flat at zero: request_time stayed in microseconds or milliseconds inconsistently; adjust rt_num<=500 heuristic or normalize in props.conf.

Case 4 — gc_event_count zero during known Harbor GC: wrong sourcetype or mirror_hostname mismatch between job service and nginx; align host renaming macros.

Case 5 — fleet_avg_p99_for_type null: mirror_type missing after join; repair registry_mirror_inventory.csv and confirm eventstats sees multiple mirrors per type.

Case 6 — low_mirror_inventory_drift for legitimate canary hosts: add canary rows to the lookup with expected_cache_hit_pct set to zero or mark mirror_type experimental in governance documentation.

Case 7 — join multiplies rows: duplicate mirror_hostname lines in CSV; deduplicate inputlookup with | dedup mirror_hostname before join.

Case 8 — severity never reaches critical on soft 5xx bursts: http_req denominator tiny; require minimum http_req threshold in a wrapper macro before paging.

Case 9 — ECR pull-through mirrors missing artifactory:request: ingest CloudWatch or application load balancer logs into the same index with mirrored field aliases so arm=http logic still applies.

Dashboard layout: mirror p99 heatmap by mirror_type, cache_hit_pct time series with expected_cache_hit_from_baseline ribbon, top ten mirrors by http_5xx_rate_pct bar chart, and disk_fill_pct trend lines split by upstream_registry.

Evidence: weekly CSV export of the closing table with lookup git hash, saved search description updated quarterly, and links to Harbor or Artifactory change tickets when thresholds move.

Case 10 — False critical from bot scanners: internet-facing mirrors probed with junk URIs can inflate 5xx; filter source IP allow lists in a wrapper macro tied to your WAF.

Case 11 — Nexus repository format differences: Docker format versus raw format hosts share one JVM but different connectors; split mirror_type in inventory so eventstats does not blend incomparable mirrors.

When finance questions spend on extra blob storage, translate disk_fill_pct trends into projected months-to-full using linear regression on weekly averages and attach the regression chart to the same evidence folder as this search export. When application teams blame mirrors for slow deploys, overlay Argo CD or Spinnaker timestamps on cache_hit_pct charts to show whether promotion waves align with MISS spikes. For multi-region active-active mirrors, duplicate mirror_hostname keys with a region column in the lookup and extend this SPL later with eventstats BY region so you do not average Singapore latency with Virginia latency. Document which Harbor version introduced structured JSON job logs versus plain text so field extractions stay version-pinned. When Artifactory adds new access log fields, regression-test perc99 calculations because string-typed durations silently break math. For Nexus, watch disk growth on the OrientDB or H2 volumes separately from blob storage; this use case focuses on layer storage mounts but teams must not conflate metadata DB fill with blob fill. Export monthly sums of http_req per mirror to compare with CDN or private-link bills so cost anomalies trace to traffic rather than to mis-tagged resources. When mirrors terminate mTLS for robot accounts, ensure 401 and 403 rates route to identity runbooks by cloning severity branches rather than lumping all 4xx into noise, even though this baseline SPL emphasizes 5xx and latency.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In lab, replay harbor:nginx:access fixtures where fifty-one percent of rows carry HTTP 503 for mirror_hostname lab-mirror-01 while linux:df shows normal fill; run container_uc_3_1_20_registry_mirror_health and expect critical_mirror_5xx_rate_above_5pct with elevated http_5xx_rate_pct.
controlTest.negativeScenario: In lab, ingest only healthy 200/304 responses with balanced HIT markers and stable disk_fill_pct under fifty percent for a mirror fully described in registry_mirror_inventory.csv; expect null severity rows filtered out by where isnotnull(severity) so no page fires.
